name: Indian Dribble Relay

phases.name: Setup
phases.instructions: Place 6 cones in a line, 1 meter apart. Players line up behind the first cone.
coachingPoints: Ensure enough space between lanes for safety

phases.name: Demonstration
phases.instructions: Coach demonstrates the Indian dribble: alternating forehand and reverse stick while moving the ball side-to-side through the cones. Keep the ball close, head up.

phases.name: Practice
phases.instructions: Players dribble through the cones using the Indian dribble technique. Walk pace first, then increase speed. Repeat 4-5 times.

objectives: Build comfort switching forehand to reverse
variations: Add a turn at the end
Blindfold one eye for advanced players
safetyNotes: Ensure sticks stay below knee height

youtubeScript.hook: Want silky smooth stick skills? The Indian dribble is THE foundational move every field hockey player needs to master.
youtubeScript.demo: Watch how I keep the ball glued to my stick, alternating forehand and reverse through each cone. The secret is soft hands and a low body position.
keyPoints: Keep hands apart on the stick for control
Roll the wrist — don't chop at the ball
youtubeScript.callToAction: Practice this for 5 minutes every day and you'll see massive improvement in just one week. Drop a comment with your best time!

name: Push Pass Pairs

phases.name: Setup
phases.instructions: Players pair up facing each other, 5 meters apart. Place a cone between them as a target line.
coachingPoints: Adjust distance for age group — closer for U6/U8

phases.name: Demonstration
phases.instructions: Coach demonstrates the push pass: left foot forward, ball at right hip, push through the ball with the flat side of the stick. Follow through toward the target.
coachingPoints: Hands together low on the stick

phases.name: Practice
phases.instructions: Partners push pass back and forth. Count consecutive successful passes. After 3 minutes, take one step back to increase distance.
coachingPoints: Weight of pass — firm but controllable

variations: Add a defender in the middle
safetyNotes: Watch for stray balls from other pairs

youtubeScript.hook: The push pass is the most used skill in field hockey. Here's how to nail it every time.
youtubeScript.demo: Step with your left foot toward your target, get low, and push through the ball. Watch the follow-through — that's where accuracy comes from.
keyPoints: Left foot forward pointing at target
youtubeScript.callToAction: Grab a friend and try 50 passes each. Comment how many you got in a row!

name: Trap and Turn

phases.name: Setup
phases.instructions: Set up a 10x10m grid. Player A starts at one end with the ball, Player B at the other end facing away.
coachingPoints: Grid size adjustable based on skill level

phases.name: Execution
phases.instructions: Player A passes to Player B. On receiving, B must trap the ball, execute a 180° turn (forehand or reverse), and dribble to the end line. Alternate roles every 3 reps.
coachingPoints: Cushion the ball on the trap — don't stab at it
Turn on the open side away from imaginary defender

phases.name: Progression
phases.instructions: Add a passive defender. The receiver must scan before the ball arrives, choose which side to turn, and escape the defender.
coachingPoints: Body position: side-on to see the field
Quick first touch out of the turn

variations: Receiver calls which direction they'll turn
safetyNotes: Defenders must not lunge — passive pressure only in early stages

name: Circle Entry & Shoot

phases.name: Setup
phases.instructions: Use the shooting circle (or mark one with cones). Set up 3 feeders at 45° left, center top, and 45° right of the circle. Shooter starts at the circle edge.
coachingPoints: Ensure GK is padded up if using live keeper

phases.name: Pattern 1 - Straight
phases.instructions: Feeder plays ball into the circle from the top. Shooter receives, takes one touch to set, and shoots. Focus on hitting the ball flat and low.
coachingPoints: Watch the ball onto the stick
Aim for corners — low and hard

phases.name: Pattern 2 - Angle
phases.instructions: Ball fed from the right. Shooter leads into the circle, receives on the move, and shoots first-time or with one touch.
coachingPoints: Open body to see goal and ball
Deflections count — stick on the ground

phases.name: Pattern 3 - Reverse
phases.instructions: Ball fed from the left. Shooter must use reverse stick to deflect or control and shoot.
coachingPoints: Redirect rather than control if possible

objectives: Develop shooting accuracy from multiple angles
variations: Timed challenge: most goals in 2 minutes
safetyNotes: All players behind the shooter when shooting
Balls must be hit below backboard height

name: Jab & Block Tackle

phases.name: Setup
phases.instructions: Mark a 5m-wide channel with cones, 15m long. One attacker, one defender. Attacker tries to dribble through; defender tries to win the ball.
coachingPoints: Defender starts 5m back from the attacker

phases.name: Jab Tackle
phases.instructions: Defender uses the jab tackle: extend the stick with the left hand only to poke the ball away. Focus on timing — wait for the attacker to push the ball ahead.

phases.name: Block Tackle
phases.instructions: Defender uses the block tackle: get low, present a strong flat stick on the ground. Let the attacker run the ball into your stick. Absorb and win possession.
coachingPoints: Time it as attacker pushes ball forward

variations: Attacker scores by dribbling past end line
Add a pass option for the attacker
safetyNotes: Tackle the ball, not the player

name: Stick & Ladder Agility

phases.name: Setup
phases.instructions: Lay out agility ladder. Place a ball and 3 cones 2m after the ladder end.
coachingPoints: Ladder flat on the ground, no bunching

phases.name: Footwork
phases.instructions: Players run through the ladder using quick feet (two feet in each box). At the end, pick up the stick and ball, dribble through the 3 cones, and return.
coachingPoints: Stay on the balls of your feet
Transition quickly from ladder to stick

phases.name: Progression
phases.instructions: Lateral shuffle through ladder, then receive a pass and make a quick pass back. Vary ladder patterns: icky shuffle, in-in-out-out.

variations: Carry the ball through the ladder
Add a shot at the end

phases.name: Setup
phases.instructions: Set up a 15x15m square with a cone at each corner. One player at each cone, two balls in play (at opposite corners).
coachingPoints: Start with one ball for younger players

phases.name: Pattern
phases.instructions: Player leads toward the next cone (clockwise), receives a pass on the move, carries to the cone, then passes to the next player who is leading. Continuous motion.
coachingPoints: Lead at an angle — not straight at the passer
Show your stick as a target

phases.name: Progression
phases.instructions: Change direction on coach's call. Add a central defender who tries to intercept. Players must time their leads to avoid the defender.
coachingPoints: Change of pace: slow to fast

name: GK Shot Stopping Circuit

phases.name: Setup
phases.instructions: GK in full gear in goal. Feeder with 10 balls at the top of the circle. Set out 3 markers: center, left post, right post.
coachingPoints: GK starts in ready position: weight forward, hands up, feet shoulder-width

phases.name: Central Saves
phases.instructions: Feeder hits 5 shots at body height from the top of the circle. GK focuses on getting body behind the ball, logging or using kickers.
coachingPoints: Watch the ball onto your equipment
Clear to the sides, not back to the shooter

phases.name: Angle Saves
phases.instructions: Feeder moves to the right, then left. GK must shuffle across goal to cut the angle. 3 shots from each side.
coachingPoints: Stay big — don't go to ground early
Lead with the hand on the ball side

phases.name: Reaction
phases.instructions: Feeder plays a pass across the circle to a second shooter for a deflection/first-time shot. GK must react and save.
coachingPoints: Stay on your feet as long as possible
Recovery: get up fast after a save

variations: Add a 1v1 breakaway after the circuit
Coach calls which side to dive
safetyNotes: Shooters wait for GK to be ready
No raised shots above shoulder without GK permission

name: Cone Slalom Speed Dribble

phases.name: Setup
phases.instructions: Place 8 cones in a zigzag pattern, 2 meters apart. Players line up at the start.
coachingPoints: Wider spacing for younger age groups

phases.name: Walk Through
phases.instructions: Players walk through the cones, keeping the ball on the forehand side of each cone. Focus on stick angle and ball position.
coachingPoints: Stick angled to steer the ball
Ball never more than one stick length away

phases.name: Speed Challenge
phases.instructions: Players go at increasing speed. Time each run. Challenge: can you beat your previous time without losing the ball?
coachingPoints: Look ahead, not at your feet
Use the edges of the stick

objectives: Develop close ball control at speed
safetyNotes: One player at a time per lane

youtubeScript.hook: This is the BEST drill to improve your dribbling as a beginner. You just need 8 cones and a ball!
youtubeScript.demo: Weave through each cone, keeping the ball tight to your stick. Start slow and build up speed. Watch my stick angle — that's the key!
keyPoints: Speed comes from control, not rushing
youtubeScript.callToAction: Set up your cones at home and send me your fastest time!

name: 3v2 Overload Attack

phases.name: Setup
phases.instructions: Mark a 30x20m area with a goal at one end. 3 attackers (pinnies) vs 2 defenders + GK. Ball starts with central attacker at halfway.

phases.name: Play
phases.instructions: Attackers try to score. They must make at least 2 passes before shooting. Defenders try to win the ball and clear it past the halfway line. Reset after each attempt.
coachingPoints: Attackers: create the 2v1 — find the free player
Ball speed: pass the ball faster than you can run it
Width and depth: triangles, not lines

phases.name: Rotation
phases.instructions: After every 4 attacks, rotate: 2 attackers become defenders, 2 defenders join the next attacking group. Keep score across rotations.

objectives: Apply passing skills in game-like overload
Develop decision-making: when to pass, carry, or shoot
Teach defensive principles: delay and channel
variations: Add a time limit for each attack (10 seconds)
Must score from inside the circle

name: Home Wall Ball Skills

phases.name: Setup
phases.instructions: Find a solid wall (garage, side of house). Stand 3 meters away. Use a smooth ball (indoor ball ideal on concrete).

phases.name: Push Pass to Wall
phases.instructions: Push pass the ball into the wall and receive the rebound. 50 reps. Focus on consistent technique and clean first touch on the return.
coachingPoints: Cushion the rebound — don't let it bounce off your stick

phases.name: One-Touch Challenge
phases.instructions: Try to keep a rally going with one touch only — pass and receive in one motion. Count your streak. Target: 10 in a row.
coachingPoints: Angle your stick to direct the ball

objectives: Enable training at home without teammates
variations: Set targets on the wall with chalk
safetyNotes: Use an indoor ball on hard surfaces
Avoid walls near windows or cars

youtubeScript.hook: No teammates? No problem! Here's how to train your passing at home with just a wall.
youtubeScript.demo: Push pass into the wall, cushion the rebound, and go again. Simple but SO effective. Watch how I angle my stick for the one-touch drill.
keyPoints: One-touch challenge: beat your record daily
youtubeScript.callToAction: What's your best one-touch streak? Mine is 27. Try to beat it and comment below!

name: Hockey Freeze Tag

phases.name: Setup
phases.instructions: Mark a 20x20m area. Everyone has a ball and stick. Choose 2 taggers (wear pinnies). Taggers do NOT have a ball.
coachingPoints: Adjust grid size for number of players

phases.name: Play
phases.instructions: Taggers try to tag dribblers. If tagged, you freeze in a star shape. To be unfrozen, a free player must pass their ball through the frozen player's legs. Play for 90 seconds then switch taggers.
coachingPoints: Keep the ball close when evading

phases.name: Debrief
phases.instructions: Gather the group. Ask: what worked to avoid the taggers? (changing speed, direction). Who helped unfreeze the most friends? Celebrate teamwork.
coachingPoints: Praise effort and teamwork over speed

objectives: Introduce dribbling in a fun game context
variations: Everyone has a ball including taggers
Two teams: most freezes in 90 seconds wins
safetyNotes: Tag with hand on shoulder, not stick
